text:
Once upon a quiet town, there lived a lonely fox who loved collecting shiny things. Every morning he'd go out into the forest, searching for glittering glass shards that seemed to catch the sun's light.

One day he found a mysterious bottle with a beautiful picture on it. The fox tucked it away into his densicle treasury of collectibles. Every time he looked at the picture, he imagined himself riding a unicorn made of glittering opals across a field of lavender.

As weeks turned into months, the fox amassed a collection of shining treasures.

Then one day, a wise old owl perched on a oak tree watched him from a branch.

"Oh dear," the owl hooted in a gentle, melodious voice. "Always eager for more glitter."

The fox hesitated, his glitter-covered paw shaking.

The owl swooped down and gently held the glittering treasure aloft.

"You've got plenty," the owl exclaimed as it fluttered back into the tree. "Take my
